channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: The Baltic undersea cable cutting highlights the internet’s underlying vulnerabilities
item.description: The severing of a key subsea telecoms cable running under Europe’s Baltic Sea has caused a diplomatic incident this week, as both country’s politicians called the act “sabotage”—all while Russia, whom many say is the likely instigator, denies responsibility.
item.dc:creator: Chris Stokel-Walker, Fast Company

item.title: Comcast to spin off cable networks, once star performers for the entertainment giant
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Comcast will spin off many of its cable television networks that were once at the heart of the entertainment giant, with people increasingly swapping out their cable TV subscriptions for streaming platforms.
item.dc:creator: MATT OTT, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Dangerous global botnet fueling residential proxies is being hit in major crackdown
item.description: Lumen and partners have disrupted the operations of the NSOCKS proxy and the underlying ngioweb botnet.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: US gathers allies to talk AI safety, Trump's vow to undo Biden's AI policy overshadows their work
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump has vowed to repeal President Joe Biden's signature artificial intelligence policy when he returns to the White House for a second term. What that actually means for the future of AI technology remains to be seen.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN and BARBARA ORTUTAY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Cisco: Pressure to deploy AI is up, but only 13% feel ready
item.description: Pressure to implement AI plans is on the rise, but the readiness of enterprise networks to handle AI workloads has actually declined over the past year, according to new research from Cisco.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: You’ll soon be able to clone your voice to speak other languages ​​in Teams
item.description: In connection with this year’s Ignite conference, Microsoft has unveiled a new interpretation tool that will be added to Teams in the spring.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Meta Hires Former Salesforce CEO to Lead Business AI Group
item.description: Meta has hired Clara Shih, former CEO of Salesforce AI, to lead a new business AI group. The move marks a shift in the company's focus towards commercializing its Llama large language models.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Ukraine has seen success in building clean energy, which is harder for Russia to destroy
item.description: This year, Russian missile attacks have knocked out about 50% of Ukraine's power generation, a major threat as winter looms.
item.dc:creator: Peter Prengaman, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Microsoft extends Entra ID to WSL, WinGet
item.description: Microsoft has added new security features to Windows Subsystem for Linux (WSL) and the Windows Package Manager (WinGet), including integration with Microsoft Entra ID (formerly Active Directory) for identity-based access control.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: New eagle camera is going live in Minnesota to the delight of global viewers
item.description: Eagle lovers around the world who were crushed when the nest of a Minnesota pair and their chick collapsed on live video last year will get a when a new eagle camera goes live this week.
item.dc:creator: Steve Karnowski, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: The 5 next big things in AR, VR, spatial computing, and multiverse for 2024
item.description: No, the Apple Vision Pro didn’t prove to be the iPhone of AR, but augmented and virtual experiences got a fresh jolt of energy in 2024 all the same.
item.dc:creator: Mark Wilson, Fast Company

item.title: Microsoft pitches AI 'agents' that can perform tasks on their own at Ignite 2024
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — Microsoft CEO Satya Nadella told customers at a conference in Chicago on Tuesday that the company is teaching a new set of artificial intelligence tools how to "act on our behalf across our work and life.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Forget driverless cars. One company wants autonomous helicopters to spray crops and fight fires
item.description: HENNIKER, N.H. (AP) — When Hector Xu was learning to fly a helicopter in college, he recalled having a few “nasty experiences” while trying to navigate at night.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Instagram rolls out option to reset recommendations
item.description: Instagram is testing a new feature that allows users to reset their content recommendations on the platform when in need of a "fresh start.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: German police break up an environmental activists' camp near Tesla plant outside Berlin
item.description: BERLIN (AP) — German police broke up a protest camp housing environmental activists in a forest near the Tesla electric car factory in Grünheide outside Berlin on Tuesday, citing violations of public safety and order.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: AI chatbot reportedly says to eat deadly mushrooms
item.description: “FungiFriend” was added to a popular mushroom discussion Facebook group and encouraged members to “sauté in butter” a potentially dangerous mushroom.
item.dc:creator: Liz Jassin, NewsNation

item.title: Rust Foundation moves forward on C++ and Rust interoperability
item.description: The Rust Foundation, which stewards development of the Rust language, has released a statement addressing challenges and opportunities for interoperability between Rust and C++, with the intent of making cross-language development more accessible and approachable.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: In Cyprus, Ukrainians learn how to dispose of landmines that kill and maim hundreds
item.description: NICOSIA, Cyprus (AP) — In a Cypriot National Guard camp, Ukrainians are being trained on how to identify, locate and dispose of landmines and other unexploded munitions that litter huge swaths of their country, killing and maiming hundreds of people, including children.
item.dc:creator: MENELAOS HADJICOSTIS, Associated Press, ABC 4
